continuation_of: GFX-PKT-006C-R4 — Post-Merge Exact-Time and Quarantine Provenance Closure v0.3 (PR #35, same branch)
authorising_review: GFX-EVD-006C-R4 — PM Review and Exact-Instant Factuality Remediation Required v0.1

prior_defects_corrected.decimal_string_conversion_limit: R4 v0.3 parse_canonical_utc_instant used Fraction(int(digits), 10 ** len(digits)); int(digits) depends on CPython's int<->str digit limit (default 4300), so a schema-valid fraction with more digits would raise a raw ValueError. Corrected: the fraction is stored as a normalized decimal-digit string (trailing zeros removed; '' == whole second) and compared lexicographically; no int()/10**n/float is ever formed, so arbitrary-length fractions parse and order correctly.
prior_defects_corrected.mutability_overstatement: R4 v0.3 described UtcInstant as 'immutable' but it used plain __slots__ whose attributes were assignable. Corrected: __setattr__/__delattr__ now raise; state is set only via object.__setattr__ in a validating constructor.
prior_defects_corrected.hash_equality_inconsistency: R4 v0.3 defined __hash__ = hash((epoch, frac)) while __eq__ compared equal to bare non-boolean integer epochs, so equal objects (instant vs int) could hash differently — a data-model contract violation. Corrected: __hash__ = None (deliberately unhashable) while exact equality/order with another UtcInstant and with bare integer epochs is retained.
prior_defects_corrected.arbitrary_precision_overstatement: R4 v0.3 evidence/docs called the representation 'arbitrary-precision'; with the int-conversion limit it was not. The implementation is now genuinely arbitrary-length-safe and the wording is corrected.
prior_defects_corrected.file_count_overstatement: R4 v0.3 evidence prose said '16 authorised files changed vs base'; the independently verified R4 v0.3 PR diff (base 6dfe22a..C10 6cedad0) is 17 files. This manifest records the correct count of 17. The R4 manifest's prior bytes are preserved; only an additive supersession pointer is added.

files_unchanged_authorised: tools/research_smoke.py (no interface change required; left unchanged)

remediation_results.normalized_decimal_representation: UtcInstant stores (int epoch second, normalized fractional digit string). parse_canonical_utc_instant normalizes via digits.rstrip('0'); leading zeros preserved. Comparison uses native tuple/str ordering: for trailing-zero-normalized fractional strings, lexicographic order equals numeric order (a shorter string that is a prefix is numerically smaller because a normalized string never ends in a zero). No int()/10**n/float; no dependence on sys.set_int_max_str_digits.
remediation_results.immutability: __setattr__ and __delattr__ raise AttributeError; state is written only by object.__setattr__ in a constructor that rejects non-int epoch, non-str fraction, or a non-normalized fraction (non-digit or trailing-zero). repr contains only epoch + normalized digits (synthetic; no input path/data).
remediation_results.unhashable: __hash__ = None — hash(instant) raises TypeError and the value cannot be placed in a set/dict, while equality/order with another UtcInstant and with bare non-boolean integer epochs is exact.
remediation_results.use_sites_transparent: timezone.validate_timezone_evidence/gate_for_normalisation, tools/research_smoke._parse_utc (availability-vs-observation ordering) and storage._parse_manifest_instant continue to use the value purely for comparison; none hashes it; all remain green.
remediation_results.governed_invalid_input: non-string, non-canonical, impossible calendar/time and non-UTC inputs still raise ContractError (timezone/research) or StorageError (manifest); a raw ValueError never escapes.

tests.exact_representation_matrix: TestExactInstantPrimitive R4-R1 additions: 10,000-digit fraction parses without raw/governed failure; two 10,000-digit fractions differing at the final digit order correctly; a 10,000-zero fraction equals the whole-second timestamp and the integer epoch; long leading-zero fractions order correctly (more leading zeros => smaller); no int conversion-limit ValueError escapes; .1Z == .10Z == .100Z; seventh-digit/ms/us cases remain green; assignment/deletion of internal state raises AttributeError; hash(instant) raises TypeError and {instant} raises; whole-second equality/order with an integer epoch correct; impossible/noncanonical input raises ContractError.
tests.regression: All prior R4 quarantine-provenance, publication, timezone, research, storage, concurrency, HTTP, security, Parquet/DuckDB and CI behavior remain green; research and market-data smokes PASS and are byte-for-byte deterministic across two runs.

Every admitted digit participates in ordering; trailing-zero forms are equal
The type is unhashable while remaining equal/ordered with integer epochs
Governed invalid-input behavior retained; all existing use sites green
Repository wording is lifecycle-neutral and factual
Prior R4 evidence changes by only the additive supersession pointer; the R4 PR file count is 17
All five CI jobs pass for the C11 (and C12) push + PR runs

research smoke + market-data smoke: PASS; each byte-for-byte deterministic across two runs
